text.tspan: WonderDrive — one answer screen, two states
Loading happens inside the information layout; the page never swaps to a separate research screen.
Same URL · research fills the answer panel
Paths appear when the sourced answer is ready.
Same URL · content replaces its placeholders
ities remember in layers: through street names, buildings,
archives, rituals, and the stories residents repeat. Official
records preserve decisions, while everyday memory often survives
in landmarks, local language, and contested public space.
What a city chooses to preserve—and what it allows to disappear—
reveals who has had the power to define its shared past.
A city's memory is less like a vault and more like a
negotiation that every generation quietly edits.
The question header, answer column, and direction rail mount once. During research, the answer column shows observable activity and content-shaped
placeholders while paths remain visibly disabled. When validation succeeds, information and choices replace those regions without a page change.
